prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG cyberpunk world atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Shadowrun / Sixth World inspired cyberpunk urban-fantasy map, continent plus archipelago terrain type.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye neon cartographic view of one dominant future-sprawl continent with a large uneven offshore archipelago.
Subject: one main continent with complex coastlines, corporate harbor gulfs, bays, straits, broken industrial coasts, polluted river deltas, inland seas, and many irregular island chains offshore. Islands must be unevenly distributed with varied sizes and shapes: arcology islands, tiny smuggling islets, volcanic islands, reef rings, lagoons, narrow channels, remote atolls, corporate black-site islands, awakened wilderness islands, and barrens slum islands. Include megacorp arcology towers, barrens districts, smuggling docks, matrix hubs, gang territory overlays, awakened forest preserves, desert badlands, mountains, wetlands, tundra, and coastal zones.
Style/medium: cyberpunk city-district map expanded to continent scale, matrix overlay hexagons, neon highlights on dark atlas, rain-slick dystopian cartography, high-detail illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, dominant continent offset to one side and archipelago sweeping diagonally around it, no simple grid spacing, full map visible.
Lighting/mood: neon-soaked corporate dystopia with street magic, perpetual night, rain haze, corporate surveillance, smuggler sea-lane tension.
Color palette: black and charcoal base, neon cyan, magenta, acid green, amber hazard lights, deep blue ocean, muted biome colors beneath glowing overlays.
Materials/textures: wet asphalt sheen, holographic matrix grid, engraved coastlines, digital scan lines, translucent district overlays.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Islands should feel natural and irregular, not evenly spaced.
